question: Based on Tables 5.2-2 and 5.3-1, is the RSV capacity adequate for overpressure protection?

nuscale_ch05_0007.title: Integrity of Reactor Coolant Boundary
nuscale_ch05_0007.summary: This section describes the design features of each NuScale Power Module (NPM) that ensure the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) throughout the plant’s design life. It details the components and systems included in the RCPB, such as the reactor pressure vessel, containment isolation valves, safety valves, and associated piping, all designed to meet regulatory definitions and maintain pressure containment.

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report regarding the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary. It includes guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as tables summarizing relevant ASME code cases for reactor components.

nuscale_ch05_0009.title: Overpressure Protection
nuscale_ch05_0009.summary: This section outlines NuScale's compliance with overpressure protection requirements by referencing applicable ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) and Operations and Maintenance (OM) code cases, as mandated by various sections of 10 CFR 50.55a and related Regulatory Guides (RGs). It details how code cases for design, fabrication, construction, preservice, and inservice inspections and testing are selected and verified to meet NRC requirements, with a consolidated list provided in Table 5.2-1.

nuscale_ch05_0008.title: Compliance with Codes and Code Cases
nuscale_ch05_0008.summary: This section describes how the NuScale Power Module (NPM) and its reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) components comply with relevant U.S. regulations, specifically 10 CFR 50.55a and associated General Design Criteria. It confirms that these components are designed, fabricated, and tested to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) Section III, 2013 Edition standards, and outlines the classification of connected piping and systems according to regulatory and quality assurance requirements.

nuscale_ch05_0017.title: Reactor Vessel Integrity
nuscale_ch05_0017.summary: This section outlines the procedures and regulatory compliance measures in place to ensure the integrity of the NuScale reactor vessel throughout its 60-year design life. It details the development of operating procedures to maintain pressure-temperature limits, evaluates the vessel’s resistance to pressurized thermal shock and upper-shelf energy degradation, and confirms that all predicted values remain within regulatory safety criteria.

nuscale_ch05_0014.title: Reactor Vessel
nuscale_ch05_0014.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module’s reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single vessel surrounded by a containment vessel. The RPV serves as a key pressure boundary to prevent the release of fission products and provides structural support for various reactor components, with all materials and fabrication processes adhering to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code standards to ensure long-term integrity.

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the NuScale pressurizer, including the RPV upper shell, baffle plate, heater bundles, and associated components. It specifies the types of steel and nickel-based alloys used, their protective claddings, and relevant material standards to ensure corrosion resistance and compatibility with operating environments.

nuscale_ch05_0019.title: Reactor Coolant System Component and Subsystem Design
nuscale_ch05_0019.summary: This section describes the design of the Reactor Coolant System (RCS) components and subsystems in the NuScale Power Module, including the reactor pressure vessel, steam generators, safety and emergency valves, and associated piping. It details the integration of the steam generators within the reactor vessel, the separation of primary and secondary sides, and references other sections for specific component design bases.

nuscale_ch05_0021.title: Reactor Coolant System Piping
nuscale_ch05_0021.summary: The section discusses the design and degradation assessment of the NuScale reactor coolant system's steam generator (SG) tubing. It notes that the main degradation observed in similar operating reactors is wear from flow-induced vibration or foreign objects, and that NuScale's design—with its helical tubing and novel support structure—does not increase risk compared to existing designs. Prototypic testing and standard SG program guidelines are deemed sufficient, with wear remaining the most likely degradation mechanism and corrosion risks assessed as low.

nuscale_ch05_0003.title: Design Basis
nuscale_ch05_0003.summary: The Design Basis section outlines that the NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping, ensuring safe and efficient heat transfer from the reactor core to the steam generator. The RCS is designed to maintain proper coolant flow, water chemistry, and boron concentration to protect fuel integrity and support safe operation under normal and transient conditions.

edges.description: Section A describes the design features and regulatory definitions ensuring the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, while Section B lists references that provide supporting standards and guidelines for those design features.

Section A establishes the requirement to maintain the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, while Section B references compliance with ASME and NRC overpressure protection codes that specify how this integrity is ensured, thus Section B provides the regulatory framework that supports the claim in Section A.

Section A describes the design intent to maintain the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, while Section B specifies that this integrity is achieved by complying with regulatory codes and standards such as 10 CFR 50.55a and ASME BPVC.

Section A defines the design features and regulatory boundaries for reactor coolant pressure integrity, while Section B discusses the development of operating procedures to ensure pressure-temperature limits are not exceeded, both connecting through the requirement to maintain RCPB integrity under all conditions.

Section B identifies the reactor vessel as a pressure-retaining component of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, which Section A describes as requiring integrity for overpressure protection, thus connecting the vessel's design to the boundary's regulatory requirements.
Section A provides references to industry standards and guidelines that underpin the regulatory and technical basis for overpressure protection requirements discussed in Section B.

Section A lists references, including ASME and EPRI standards, that are cited to support the compliance claims made in Section B regarding adherence to regulatory codes for reactor coolant boundary integrity.

Section A provides references that support the technical basis for reactor vessel integrity, while Section B discusses the development of operating procedures to ensure compliance with pressure-temperature limits, both of which are connected through the requirement to maintain vessel integrity under overpressure conditions.

Section A provides references to regulatory and technical standards that underpin the design and analysis described in Section B, which details the reactor vessel's structure and function relevant to overpressure protection.

Section A describes the use of ASME BPVC code cases and regulatory provisions for overpressure protection, while Section B establishes that the design and construction comply with 10 CFR 50.55a and ASME BPVC requirements, thus Section A provides specific implementation details that support the general compliance claim in Section B.

Section A outlines the regulatory and code compliance framework for overpressure protection, while Section B describes the development of operating procedures to ensure pressure-temperature limits are not exceeded, both connecting through their shared goal of maintaining reactor vessel integrity under overpressure scenarios.

Section A outlines the regulatory and code compliance requirements for overpressure protection, while Section B describes the reactor vessel as the pressure-retaining component subject to those requirements, connecting them through the need for the vessel's overpressure protection to comply with the specified codes.

Section A establishes that the reactor pressure vessel and associated components comply with ASME Code requirements for integrity, while Section B describes the development of operating procedures to ensure pressure-temperature limits are not exceeded, both contributing to the assurance that overpressure protection (including RSV capacity) meets regulatory and operational requirements.

Section A establishes that the reactor pressure vessel (RPV) and associated pressure-retaining components comply with ASME Code requirements for overpressure protection, while Section B describes the RPV as a key pressure-retaining component within the reactor coolant system, thus connecting the regulatory compliance in A to the physical system described in B.

Section A discusses the development of operating procedures to ensure pressure-temperature limits are not exceeded, which directly supports the reactor vessel's overpressure protection function described in Section B.
Section A specifies the materials and components of the pressurizer, which are part of the reactor coolant pressure boundary whose integrity and design features are described in Section B.
Section A references Section B by directing the reader to Section 5.2 for material descriptions of the RPV upper shell and upper head, which are detailed in Section B.

Section A describes the materials and components of the pressurizer, which is a part of the reactor coolant system, while Section B outlines the regulatory codes and standards (ASME BPVC, 10 CFR 50.55a) that these components must comply with for overpressure protection, connecting them through the requirement that the pressurizer's design and materials must meet the specified regulatory provisions.

Section A describes the design and components of the reactor coolant system, including the RSVs, while Section B outlines the regulatory requirement to maintain the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, which the RSVs help protect by providing overpressure protection.

Section A describes the design and components of the reactor coolant system, including the RSVs whose adequacy is questioned, while Section B lists references that provide standards and guidelines relevant to the integrity and requirements for these components.

Section A describes the inclusion and design of reactor safety valves (RSVs) as part of the reactor coolant system, while Section B outlines the regulatory requirements and code compliance for overpressure protection, connecting them through the necessity for RSVs to meet these regulatory standards.

Section A describes the design and degradation mechanisms of reactor coolant system piping, which are components included in the reactor coolant pressure boundary whose integrity is addressed in Section B.

Section A discusses the observed degradation mechanisms and risk assessment for SG tubing in the reactor coolant system, while Section B lists references that provide supporting technical standards and guidelines relevant to the integrity and reliability of reactor coolant boundary materials, connecting them through the use of referenced industry standards to justify design choices.

Section B establishes the regulatory requirements and codes for overpressure protection that the RSV capacity must comply with, while Section A discusses the design and operational experience of the reactor coolant system components, which must satisfy those requirements.

Section A describes the components and configuration of the reactor coolant system, which Section B references as part of the reactor coolant pressure boundary whose integrity must be maintained according to regulatory definitions.

Section A provides a general description of the reactor coolant system design, which is the system subject to the overpressure protection requirements referenced in Section B, where Section B lists standards and guidelines that inform the integrity and quality assurance of that system.

Section A describes the design basis and configuration of the reactor coolant system, which is the system requiring overpressure protection, while Section B outlines the regulatory codes and standards (ASME BPVC, 10 CFR 50.55a) that the overpressure protection provisions, such as RSV capacity, must comply with.

Section A provides specific operational experience and degradation mechanisms for steam generator tubing relevant to the reactor coolant system, while Section B gives a general summary of the reactor coolant system design, connecting Section A's details as supporting evidence for the adequacy of the system described in Section B.
